title: acmella oleracea
intro: Acmella oleracea, also known as the toothache plant, is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve pain and reduce swelling. The medicinal properties of Acmella oleracea are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Acmella oleracea can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat toothaches, gum inflammation, and other oral health issues.
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", acmella oleracea benefits include its potential as an antidiabetic agent through α-glucosidase inhibition, which may aid in controlling hyperglycaemia in type-2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM).
